title: Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Elena Castellini from Brookline will run her first Boston Marathon in 2026, supporting the One World Strong Foundation.
The One World Strong Foundation aids survivors of trauma, founded by 2013 marathon bombing survivor Dave Fortier.
Elena Castellini is fundraising for victims in Ukraine, providing medical aid and psychological support through her marathon effort.

title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter for allegedly stabbing boyfriend Kyle Bradford, 26, during an argument in South Boston.
The incident occurred at 258 Old Colony Ave.; Bradford was found with a stab wound and later died at the hospital.
Pascual's lawyer claims self-defense, citing Bradford's past criminal cases and threats made against Pascual before the stabbing.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will feature on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the 2027 season, a franchise first.
HBO's 'Hard Knocks' will follow the Seattle Seahawks during training camp ahead of the 2026 season.
Changes in NFL rules now allow playoff teams like the Patriots to be featured on 'Hard Knocks.'

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first professional goal in Providence Bruins' 6-4 win over the Bridgeport Islanders.
Hagens recorded an assist in Providence Bruins' 4-2 loss to the Springfield Thunderbirds, extending his point streak.
Hagens is on an AHL amateur tryout contract after finishing his sophomore season at Boston College.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to his $26.8 million cap hit despite his productive 2025 season.
Team EVP Eliot Wolf has not ruled out a reunion with Diggs, citing potential scenarios like injuries or scheme changes.
Diggs remains unsigned three weeks into free agency; Patriots may consider him as an option if other plans fail.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was sentenced to at least 50 years for raping two women in Framingham in 1989.
Convicted of multiple charges including aggravated rape and armed robbery, Gale's case was solved using genetic genealogy.
Victims delivered impact statements during sentencing, and Gale's attorney filed a notice of appeal following the decision.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning Coach of the Year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla criticized the NBA Coach of the Year award as a 'stupid award', focusing instead on team efforts.
The Celtics secured their fifth-consecutive 50-win season, ranking second in the Eastern Conference despite missing Jayson Tatum.
Mazzulla emphasizes caring for players over basketball itself, highlighting the importance of influencing and helping team members.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person died in a car crash in Mattapan late Sunday night, police reported.
The crash happened at 11:14 p.m. on American Legion Highway near Walk Hill and Canterbury Streets.
Officers found the victim in the car on the sidewalk; extensive front and driver side damage was noted.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla is a strong contender for NBA Coach of the Year with second-best odds, behind Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
Mazzulla led the Celtics to a 50-24 record despite losing key players, ranking in the top-four in both offensive and defensive ratings.
The Celtics have improved in key areas like rebounding and turnovers, demonstrating development and resilience under Mazzulla's leadership.

title: Ice sculpture carved for Boston’s ‘No Kings’ rally destroyed by vandals, organizers say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: An 'End ICE' ice sculpture was vandalized on Boston Common after the 'No Kings' rally, organizers report.
Artist Kat Dressler designed the sculpture to maintain its message even if vandalized, as the word 'ICE' was knocked over.
Police were dispatched but no report was filed; related signs promoting progressive group Activist Evenings were also destroyed.
